headline: Agent harnesses and production tooling led the quarter as the next training paradigm came into focus.
body: The quarter centered on moving AI agents from prototypes to production, with frameworks for execution harnesses [9], real-time voice agents that handle interruption [8], and large plugin ecosystems around Claude spanning code generation and task automation [2]. Dwarkesh Patel mapped the next training paradigm beyond RLHF, covering reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards and test-time compute [7]. The Big Technology AI Summit debated the federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models and whether it amounts to an AI pause, alongside infrastructure and China competition [6]. New entrants including model cascading, prompt fusion, and relevance feedback surfaced, while task decomposition and ranking carried the highest rotation ratios.
